collections.name: 00 Dimension / Primitives
collections.usage: Internal numeric source scale for spacing, radius, and border width.

collections.name: 01 Color / Opacity
collections.usage: Approved opacity modifiers only when opacity is explicitly requested or designed.

collections.name: 01 Color / Primitives
collections.usage: Raw source colors. Do not use directly in product UI.

collections.name: 01 Color / Semantic
collections.usage: Default color layer for generated product UI.

collections.name: 03 Spacing / Semantic
collections.usage: Public spacing tokens for gap, padding, and layout separation.

collections.name: 04 Radius / Semantic
collections.usage: Public radius tokens for surface shape.

collections.name: 07 Icons / Primitives
collections.usage: Icon sizing and construction reference tokens.

guardrails: Do not use primitives directly in product UI.
Do not invent raw hex, px, radius, shadow, motion, or typography values when a token exists.
Do not create component tokens during foundation validation.
Do not invent new colors, gradients, opacities, breakpoints, icons, or component-specific motion rules unless explicitly asked to extend the foundation.

Motion is an active foundation tool, not disabled; use approved motion duration and easing tokens when motion clarifies feedback, state change, loading/progress, reveal/hide, navigation continuity, or spatial relationship.
Apply motion consistently: the same intent should use the same duration, easing, direction, and reduced-motion fallback across a flow.

Component-trigger motion such as hover, press, focus, selected, or CTA animation can be suggested as a prototype assumption, but it must be labelled non-final until component specs define the behavior.
Use text.subheading.sm.medium for product UI pretitles and overlines unless a source spec requires another approved subheading weight.
Keep product UI pretitles neutral or subtle by default; do not use coral, status color, or bold pretitles unless the label has an approved meaning.
Do not repeat a generic feature label as a pretitle above every screen, panel, or card.
Choose marketing/editorial versus operational/product hierarchy before using Display scale or large spacing.
Use space.lg as the starting padding for normal product cards and panels; compact surfaces can use space.md and space.xl is for larger group separation.
Group related operational facts inside one surface before splitting every fact into a mini card.
Do not use status backgrounds to invent selected row, card, or option states.
If a prototype needs selected state before a component source spec exists, use a simple neutral structural cue and report the dependency.
Do not use coral as generic decoration, label, pretitle, status, or selected-row/card styling.
Keep Omio-branded prototype primary action emphasis on the brand role and report final action styling as a source-spec dependency.
Do not make first-level product cards border-only when elevation.surface is the clearer layer cue.
Use elevation.surface for first-level product cards or panels when they need foreground separation from color.background.default.
Keep same-level cards, panels, and sections on the same radius token unless a source spec explains the difference.
Nested surfaces should not be rounder than their parent; visibly inset children can step down one radius token, while flush children should align to the parent corner logic.
Prefer rows, dividers, spacing, or subtle background before creating nested rounded cards for related facts, metadata, rows, or comparisons.
Convert cramped tables or comparison grids into readable stacked cards or rows before text wraps into narrow fragments.
State unresolved source-spec dependencies instead of inventing foundation decisions.
Keep QA summaries and implementation notes outside user-facing UI unless explicitly requested.
